fact: June 9 is exactly 160 days into the year, which means there are exactly 205 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for fireflies to start their magical light shows in backyards and forests.
The sun rises before 5:30 AM in most northern cities around June 9, making it one of the earliest sunrise times of the entire year.
June 9 falls smack in the middle of Gemini season, when the zodiac twins are supposedly making everyone extra chatty and curious.
In Antarctica, June 9 brings some of the coldest, darkest days of winter with temperatures that can drop below -40°F!
Many fruit trees in temperate regions are busy growing tiny baby fruits around this date - apples and pears are about the size of marbles.
The summer solstice is only 12 days away from June 9, making daylight stretch longer and longer each day in the north.
In many places, June 9 marks peak allergy season when grass pollen counts hit their absolute worst levels of the year.
Ocean temperatures in the Northern Hemisphere are finally warming up enough around this time for sea turtles to start their nesting marathons on beaches.
June 9 sits right in the sweet spot when many flowers release their strongest scents to attract buzzing bees and butterflies.
In northern regions, this date brings twilight that lasts for hours - the sky stays colorful and glowy way past normal bedtime.
Many migrating whales are cruising through cooler ocean waters around June 9, following ancient routes their families have used for thousands of years.
